status.next_allowed_action: Plan complete. Do not push, merge, or amend from this goal.

decision_log.decision: Use a phased L-level ledger for Sigma/Graphology global renderer integration.
decision_log.reason: The work spans harness hardening, graph-engine renderer boundaries, production Sigma integration, route switching, workbench verification, browser performance regression, and cleanup.

decision_log.decision: Treat Phase 0 as a start gate, not a new technology selection phase.
decision_log.reason: The design names Sigma/Graphology as the preferred formal global renderer route, while requiring final evidence before production integration starts.

decision_log.decision: Start implementation on codex/sigma-global-renderer-integration with a clean-start baseline that includes the eng-review plan amendments.
decision_log.reason: The plan GSTACK REVIEW REPORT ends with NO UNRESOLVED DECISIONS, eng review is cleared after amendments, and the baseline smoke check (324 tests, 0 fail) is green. The eng-review amendments to the plan and progress files are part of this ledger, not unrelated changes.

decision_log.decision: Raise the Sigma trial hard gate to fps>=45 and frame_p95<=22.3ms for both wheel_zoom and a new sampled drag action, and require the full performance-record schema on every trial record.
decision_log.reason: Task 1.1 requires the trial to enforce the plan's hard-gate table, not the prior fps>=10 floor, and to record schema_version/production_path/thresholds/browser/build_commit/run timestamps plus mandatory fps/frame_p95/duration/memory metrics so a future false pass is blocked.

decision_log.decision: Fix the tsx/esbuild __name regression by injecting a no-op window.__name init script on every trial page, rather than disabling tsx keepNames globally.
decision_log.reason: tsx compiles with esbuild keepNames:true, injecting a __name helper into functions serialized via page.evaluate, which is undefined in the browser and broke every interaction measurement. A page-level no-op shim is the smallest change that restores the trial without weakening the build pipeline.
decision_log.source: baseline probe: nodes-1000-sparse search/point_select/container_select/drawer_open/enter_community/return_global/repeated_cycles all failed with ReferenceError: __name is not defined

decision_log.decision: Make the shared trial validator's schema/metric enforcement opt-in via requireSchema (default true); sibling vis-network and aggregation trials opt out until their own upgrade phase.
decision_log.reason: The shared validator is consumed by three trials. Hardening the schema must not break the two out-of-scope trials; opting them out keeps Task 1.1 scoped to Sigma while leaving a clear upgrade path.

decision_log.decision: Drive the Sigma trial model entirely from buildGraphRendererAdapterData output; node/edge/community/aggregation lists, positions, sizes, colors and selection/search/pin/aggregation state all come from adapter render state, not from raw GraphData traversal.
decision_log.reason: Task 1.2 forbids the trial path from deciding display budgets by traversing raw data.nodes/data.edges, so the trial proves Sigma can render from adapter-controlled data before any production Sigma code is written.

decision_log.decision: Use warmup + 3 repeated runs and score the median fps/frame-p95 for wheel/drag, recording the worst run on each record.
decision_log.reason: The plan's hard gate requires warmup-then-3-repeated-runs with median-must-pass and worst-run-recorded. The first unwarmed wheel sample on many-pin-nodes read 35.9fps due to first-frame layout/shader cost; warmup + median eliminated the cold-start outlier while preserving honest worst-run evidence.

decision_log.decision: Introduce GraphRendererSurface as the controller command boundary for DOM/SVG focus, drag, search, selection-focus dataset, viewport-dragging, and root focus commands.
decision_log.reason: Task 2.1 requires controller intent to be expressed through a renderer-neutral surface instead of assuming every rendered node is a DOM button. The DOM/SVG implementation remains local to renderer-surface.ts, and controller no longer directly calls per-node focus/classList APIs.

decision_log.decision: Move DOM/SVG graph-content creation behind paintDomSvgGraph in dom-svg-renderer.ts while keeping pipeline responsible for orchestration, control mounting, and command wiring.
decision_log.reason: Task 2.2 requires root, pipeline, and controls boundaries to be explicit. The pipeline now calls a DOM/SVG renderer interface instead of directly creating node buttons, SVG edges, community washes, aggregation containers, minimap, reader, selection, preview, and quality notice DOM.

decision_log.decision: Keep route manager ownership in the facade and establish the Sigma runtime as an explicit graph-engine boundary: ESM workbench path loads Sigma/Graphology via lazy chunks when selected, while offline IIFE bundles the runtime in engine.iife.js.
decision_log.reason: Task 3.1 requires route ownership and bundle/offline boundaries before lifecycle work. Direct static importing Sigma into normal Node tests fails because Sigma references WebGL2RenderingContext, so the production skeleton uses an async runtime boundary instead of loading WebGL code during ordinary DOM/SVG tests.

decision_log.decision: Build the production Sigma Graphology graph only from GraphRendererAdapterData and store render-only node, edge, community, aggregation, counts, and selection attributes in Graphology.
decision_log.reason: Task 3.2 requires Sigma to consume the adapter output and not traverse raw GraphData to decide nodes, edges, labels, colors, selected/search/Pin state, or aggregation budget. Graphology is a renderer data structure, not the source of product truth.

decision_log.decision: Resolve production Sigma hits through a graph-engine bridge: Sigma node ids win first, rendered object ids map to existing graph targets, and screen-point hits project to world coordinates before using the shared spatial index.
decision_log.reason: Task 3.3 requires hit priority and community-region semantics to remain owned by graph-engine/adapter logic, not by ad hoc Sigma callback calculations.

decision_log.decision: Use a rebuild-Graphology-graph/preserve-camera update strategy for production Sigma lifecycle updates, and report initialization or unrecoverable update/destroy failures only through onFatalError.
decision_log.reason: Task 3.4 requires explicit update behavior, stale-event suppression, update-after-destroy handling, camera/state preservation, and route-layer-owned fallback decisions. Rebuilding keeps Graphology render-only while preserving Sigma camera state across adapter-driven selection/search/Pin/theme updates.

decision_log.decision: Own route switching in the facade route manager: global views select the async Sigma global renderer, community reading selects DOM/SVG, and a known unavailable Sigma route returns global through fallback without repeated retry.
decision_log.reason: Task 4.1 requires the facade to enforce the already-recorded route ownership and prove Sigma global -> DOM/SVG community -> Sigma global return behavior while preserving facade state and avoiding a user-visible renderer switch.

decision_log.decision: Keep cross-route interaction state in the facade route state, including selection, focus, search query/results, type filters, pins, aggregation markers, and temporary object payloads; facade summaries default to that state unless callers override options.
decision_log.reason: Task 4.2 requires route switches not to clear current selection and requires filtered/excluded object drawers to retain enough state to render excluded payloads consistently across Sigma and DOM/SVG routes.

decision_log.decision: Use layered Sigma failure fallback: small graphs fall back to DOM/SVG, while graphs above 2000 nodes, 4000 edges, or 500 nodes in any community go to the aggregation safety view; retry is explicit and known-failed global returns do not repeatedly recreate Sigma.
decision_log.reason: Task 4.3 requires WebGL/Sigma/runtime failures to remain usable without sending known-large graphs back to the old DOM/SVG global path, and requires repeated retry behavior to be controlled by the route layer rather than exposed as a user-visible renderer switch.

decision_log.decision: Keep renderer route details inside graph-engine/facade; workbench GraphPanel continues to use createGraphEngine, persisted pins, selection/open-page callbacks, and visibility callbacks without a user-visible renderer selector.
decision_log.reason: Task 4.4 requires workbench's public graph boundary to stay stable while the global route changes underneath the graph-engine facade.
decision_log.source: workbench/web/src/components/GraphPanel.tsx; npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki-agent/web; npm run typecheck --workspace=@llm-wiki-agent/web

decision_log.decision: Store business node type as graphNodeType in the production Sigma Graphology graph and use Sigma's supported circle node program for the renderer type field.
decision_log.reason: Task 6.1 production-path browser regression exposed that passing business types such as source/topic/entity into Sigma's type field makes Sigma fail initialization and route to fallback. The renderer type field is a Sigma drawing-program selector, not product semantics.

decision_log.decision: Rename the ambiguous DOM/SVG global fallback boundary to dom-svg-small-fallback and keep it reachable only after Sigma is unavailable for graphs below the large-graph safety threshold.
decision_log.reason: Task 7.1 requires removing old DOM/SVG global main-path branching while preserving small-graph emergency fallback, community reading, and large-graph aggregation safety fallback. The explicit route/factory names and targeted tests prevent the fallback from being mistaken for a second global main renderer.

decision_log.decision: Record the Sigma production integration result in a new docs/graph/performance result document and update user-facing release wording from future-route language to current-route language.
decision_log.reason: Task 7.2 requires result documentation to match the implemented route and progress state, including Phase 0 evidence, final route, fallback boundary, artifact paths, residual risk, and no misstatement of vis-network or aggregation as current main routes.

decision_log.decision: Close the Sigma global renderer integration plan after sequential final acceptance and final production artifact proof.
decision_log.reason: Every task is done, all acceptance commands passed, and the final production artifact proves 11 graph shapes satisfy the performance hard gates with no failed records.

decision_log.decision: Address post-review production-path evidence gaps by driving the browser regression through the real Sigma route, real search input, and real pointer selection paths, while keeping the plan closed.
decision_log.reason: The review found the production regression could pass with fake callbacks, incomplete global controls, hidden fallback, and missing loading/duration/memory hard-gate enforcement. The fix makes the regression prove the actual production route and strengthens validation without adding a user-visible old/new renderer switch.

decision_log.decision: Close ship-review blockers by hardening the real production-path validation and fixing route-state edge cases before PR creation.
decision_log.reason: The ship review found remaining correctness and evidence gaps: route fallback could retain stale data, renderer callbacks could leave facade selection stale, stale metadata could choose the wrong fallback, Sigma fixed-position commands returned false, public exports exposed Sigma internals, and production regression could pass without proving a rendered canvas or visible Sigma signal.

Sibling vis-network/aggregation trials opt out of the new schema gate (requireSchema:false) until their own upgrade; their behavior is otherwise unchanged. Wheel/drag gate raised from fps>=10 to fps>=45 + frame_p95<=22.3ms; measured headroom on this machine is ~15fps / ~5ms.

Sigma shows no hard blocker on this machine; Phase 1 start gate is cleared. Worst-case fps headroom over the 45 floor is ~15fps.

phases.title: Renderer boundary decoupling

DOM/SVG renderer root/content boundary is explicit via renderer-surface.ts and dom-svg-renderer.ts; render-pipeline delegates graph content drawing through paintDomSvgGraph and keeps command/control orchestration.

Basic keyboard and focus checks pass: keyboard + interaction-contract tests pass; lifecycle test asserts graph root is focusable, search opens focus the input, and closing search returns focus to root.

Sigma node id hits resolve directly to node targets, rendered object ids map only to known adapter objects, and screen-point hits are projected through screenPointToWorldPoint into createGraphSpatialIndex built from adapterData/renderable output. tasks.evidence: createSigmaGlobalRenderer now creates a Sigma root, builds a Graphology graph from adapter data, instantiates Sigma through the lazy runtime boundary, binds node/stage events through the graph-engine hit projector, supports update/destroy, reports initialization/update/destroy failures via onFatalError, suppresses stale events after destroy, throws on update-after-destroy, rebuilds Graphology on adapter updates, preserves camera state across updates, and carries adapter-driven selection/search/Pin/theme changes into the new graph.
tasks.residual_risk: The lifecycle is unit-proven with injected fake Sigma/Graphology runtime and real Graphology graph construction. Real browser WebGL behavior and facade route switching remain for Phase 4 and production browser regression phases.

phases.title: Global/community routing and fallback
phases.goal: Route global views to Sigma, community reading to DOM/SVG, and failures to tested fallback paths.
acceptance: Global route uses Sigma in normal operation
Community reading route uses DOM/SVG
Global Sigma to community DOM/SVG to return-global preserves selection, search, filters, and pins
Small Sigma failure falls back to DOM/SVG
Large Sigma failure falls back to aggregation safety view and known-large graphs are not sent to DOM/SVG
Fallback tests cover WebGL unavailable, Sigma initialization failure, canvas/runtime abnormal failure, retry, and known-failed no-retry behavior
Workbench GraphPanel public boundary remains stable
verification: Task 4.1 through 4.4 complete with evidence.
Global route uses Sigma in normal operation; community reading route uses DOM/SVG; Sigma -> community DOM/SVG -> return-global preserves selection/search/filter/pins in facade tests.
Small Sigma failures route to DOM/SVG global fallback; large failures route to aggregation safety fallback when nodes > 2000, edges > 4000, or max community size > 500.
Fallback tests cover WebGL unavailable, Sigma initialization failure, canvas/runtime abnormal failure, retry, and known-failed no-retry behavior.
Workbench GraphPanel boundary is stable: web tests and typecheck pass; static renderer-detail check in GraphPanel has no output.

tasks.title: Implement renderer route manager
verification: node --import tsx --test packages/graph-engine/test/facade.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/renderer-lifecycle.test.ts => exit 0 (25 tests, 0 fail).
npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine => exit 0 (340 tests, 0 fail).
npm run build --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine => exit 0.
tasks.evidence: createGraphFacade now mounts a facade-owned route manager. The normal global route creates the async Sigma global renderer from adapter data; focusCommunity switches to DOM/SVG community reading; resetView returns global through Sigma unless Sigma is already marked unavailable, in which case it uses global fallback without incrementing the Sigma attempt count. facade.test covers state preservation for selection/search/filter/pins across Sigma -> community DOM/SVG -> Sigma and covers known-unavailable no-retry fallback.
tasks.residual_risk: Task 4.1 only establishes route ownership and known-unavailable fallback routing. Full cross-route state synchronization edge cases remain in Task 4.2, and layered small/large fallback thresholds plus retry UI remain in Task 4.3.

tasks.title: Synchronize selection, search, filters, pins, and temporary objects across routes
verification: node --import tsx --test packages/graph-engine/test/state.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/search-and-legend.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/select.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/summary-contract.test.ts => exit 0 (29 tests, 0 fail).
npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine => exit 0 (342 tests, 0 fail).
npm run build --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine => exit 0.
tasks.evidence: Facade route state now carries selection, focus, searchQuery/searchResultIds, typeFilters, pins, aggregationMarkers, and temporaryObject. DOM/SVG renderer accepts an initial searchQuery so route-created community readers can restore query/highlight state. Facade summaries now default to the current facade route state, so selected/search/pinned/aggregation/excluded payloads survive route switches and filters unless callers explicitly override summary options.
tasks.residual_risk: Task 4.2 preserves and exposes shared state, but it does not define the small/large failure thresholds, aggregation safety view, or retry behavior; those remain Task 4.3.

tasks.title: Implement layered failure fallback
verification: node --import tsx --test packages/graph-engine/test/aggregation-fallback-trial-adapter.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/facade.test.ts packages/graph-engine/test/renderer-lifecycle.test.ts => exit 0 (30 tests, 0 fail).

npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine => exit 0 (346 tests, 0 fail).
tasks.evidence: Layered Sigma failure fallback is now facade-owned. Small Sigma failures route to DOM/SVG global fallback; large or known-risk graphs route to aggregation safety fallback when nodes > 2000, edges > 4000, or any community size > 500. Guard fixtures prove 2101 nodes, 4101 edges, and a 600-node community avoid DOM/SVG and render the safety view. Tests cover WebGL unavailable, synchronous Sigma initialization failure, canvas/runtime abnormal failure via onSigmaUnavailable, explicit retrySigma retry, return-global known-failed no-retry behavior, and the default aggregation safety view hint/actions.
tasks.residual_risk: Task 4.4 still needs to prove the workbench GraphPanel boundary remains stable and does not expose renderer selection. Production-path browser performance hard gates remain deferred to Phase 6/7.

turn_log.action: Added representative drawer card payload + DOM; scaled repeated memory cycles on 5000/10000; redesigned wheel/drag sampling as warmup+median-of-3 with worst-run recording.
turn_log.result: phase-1 task 1.3 complete and phase-1 acceptance met; full 11-shape trial passes all hard gates; advancing to phase 2.

turn_log.action: Introduced renderer surface command boundary and routed controller node focus, drag class, search-state, viewport-dragging, and root focus commands through it.
turn_log.result: phase-2 task 2.1 complete; graph-engine test/typecheck and renderer boundary static checks pass; advancing to task 2.2.

turn_log.action: Extracted DOM/SVG graph-content creation from render-pipeline into dom-svg-renderer and added boundary tests proving pipeline delegates graph drawing through paintDomSvgGraph.
turn_log.result: phase-2 task 2.2 complete; graph-engine test/typecheck and lifecycle/boundary checks pass; advancing to task 2.3.

turn_log.action: Added lifecycle focus assertions for root/search input/root-return and reran keyboard, interaction contract, full graph-engine test, typecheck, and controller static boundary checks.
turn_log.result: phase-2 task 2.3 complete and phase-2 acceptance met; advancing to phase 3 task 3.1.

turn_log.action: Moved Sigma/Graphology into graph-engine runtime dependencies, added Sigma global renderer skeleton/runtime boundary, exported route/bundle constants, and recorded build-size/offline evidence.
turn_log.result: phase-3 task 3.1 complete; typecheck/build/test and boundary tests pass; advancing to task 3.2.

turn_log.action: Added production Graphology graph construction from GraphRendererAdapterData and tests proving the Sigma module does not use raw GraphData for render-budget decisions.
turn_log.result: phase-3 task 3.2 complete; Sigma boundary, adapter contract, full graph-engine test, and typecheck pass; advancing to task 3.3.

turn_log.action: Added Sigma hit projection bridge from node ids, rendered object ids, and screen points into existing graph-engine gesture targets and spatial index semantics.
turn_log.result: phase-3 task 3.3 complete; Sigma hit tests, spatial/adapter/interaction tests, full graph-engine test, and typecheck pass; advancing to task 3.4.

turn_log.action: Implemented Sigma lifecycle create/update/destroy, camera-preserving Graphology rebuild updates, stale-event suppression, update-after-destroy guard, and route-layer error reporting.
turn_log.result: phase-3 task 3.4 complete and phase-3 acceptance met; Sigma tests, full graph-engine test, typecheck, build, and semantic static check pass; advancing to phase 4 task 4.1.

turn_log.action: Implemented the facade-owned renderer route manager, wired production global Sigma creation through the lazy runtime boundary, routed community reading to DOM/SVG, and added fallback no-retry behavior for known unavailable Sigma.
turn_log.result: phase-4 task 4.1 complete; required facade/lifecycle tests, full graph-engine test, and graph-engine build pass; advancing to task 4.2.

turn_log.action: Synchronized route state for selection, focus, search query/results, type filters, pins, aggregation markers, and temporary objects; wired DOM/SVG initial search query and facade summary defaults to current route state.
turn_log.result: phase-4 task 4.2 complete; required state/search/select/summary tests, full graph-engine test, and graph-engine build pass; advancing to task 4.3.

turn_log.action: Implemented layered Sigma failure fallback with small-graph DOM/SVG fallback, large-graph aggregation safety view thresholds, explicit retry, and known-failed no-retry behavior.
turn_log.result: phase-4 task 4.3 complete; fallback, lifecycle, build, and full graph-engine tests pass; advancing to task 4.4.

turn_log.action: Verified the workbench GraphPanel public boundary stayed on createGraphEngine, persisted pins, selection/open-page callbacks, and visibility callbacks without renderer route leakage.
turn_log.result: phase-4 task 4.4 complete and phase-4 acceptance met; web tests, web typecheck, GraphPanel static check, and graph-engine baseline pass; advancing to phase 5 task 5.1.

turn_log.action: Verified node, community, search, filter, and Pin semantics through interaction, search/legend, summary-contract, and full graph-engine tests.
turn_log.result: phase-5 task 5.1 complete; graph-engine interaction semantics remain stable; advancing to task 5.2.

turn_log.action: Ran web tests plus workbench and offline browser regressions for community reading, open-detail/read, return-global, selected/excluded state, and standalone HTML flows.
turn_log.result: phase-5 task 5.2 complete; workbench and offline regressions pass; advancing to task 5.3.

turn_log.action: Ran keyboard ownership tests, HTML accessibility regression, and full graph-engine tests for focus/search/Escape behavior.
turn_log.result: phase-5 task 5.3 complete; keyboard and accessibility checks pass; advancing to task 5.4.

turn_log.action: Ran community wash visual interaction regression, stage 4.5 offline browser regression, web tests, and full graph-engine tests including fallback notice coverage.
turn_log.result: phase-5 task 5.4 complete and phase-5 acceptance met; normal global, community reading/summary, and fallback state evidence recorded; advancing to phase 6 task 6.1.

turn_log.action: Added the production-path Sigma global browser regression and shell runner; fixed production Sigma node rendering type so business node types no longer make Sigma initialization fall back.
turn_log.result: phase-6 task 6.1 complete; single-shape production-path artifact proves production_path=true with full schema fields and no failed records; advancing to task 6.2.

turn_log.action: Ran the full 11-shape isolated Sigma trial and production Sigma global regression; tuned the production regression harness to measure the production global path without counting test-page setup, repeated DOM/SVG route reconstruction, or uncollected transient heap.
turn_log.result: phase-6 task 6.2 complete; isolated and production artifacts each have 110 records across 11 shapes, 0 failed records, and all hard-gate metrics within threshold; advancing to task 6.3.

turn_log.action: Ran graph-engine and web test/typecheck/build plus workbench, offline, community wash, stage-4.5 offline, and HTML accessibility browser regressions.
turn_log.result: phase-6 task 6.3 complete and phase-6 acceptance met; advancing to phase 7 task 7.1.

turn_log.action: Removed the ambiguous old DOM/SVG global fallback route name, replaced it with an explicit small-graph Sigma-unavailable fallback boundary, and added targeted route assertions for normal Sigma, DOM/SVG community, small fallback, aggregation safety fallback, and absence of renderer comparison switches.
turn_log.result: phase-7 task 7.1 complete; graph-engine test/typecheck pass; advancing to task 7.2.

turn_log.action: Added the Sigma production integration result document and updated README/CHANGELOG wording to reflect Sigma as the current global route while keeping DOM/SVG and aggregation documented only as bounded fallback/community/detail paths.
turn_log.result: phase-7 task 7.2 complete; documentation checks pass; advancing to task 7.3.

turn_log.action: Ran the complete final acceptance command list sequentially, generated the final Sigma production artifact, validated hard-gate metrics, and closed the progress ledger.
turn_log.result: plan complete; final artifact has 110 records across 11 shapes, production_path=true, 0 failures, all hard gates satisfied.

turn_log.action: Fixed post-review production-path gaps by routing the browser regression through real Sigma production controls and interactions, strengthening hard-gate validation, and rerunning targeted plus full production regressions.
turn_log.result: post-review production artifact has 110 records across 11 shapes, production_path=true, 0 failures, all required fields present, min fps 60.3, max frame p95 17.6ms, max memory growth 6MB, and loading-state evidence recorded.

turn_log.action: Fixed ship-review blockers in route manager state synchronization, stale fallback data refresh, stale metadata fallback thresholds, Sigma fixed-position commands, public Sigma export boundaries, and production regression canvas/visible-signal hard gates.
turn_log.result: ship-review production artifact has 110 records across 11 shapes, production_path=true, 0 failures, min fps 60.2, max frame p95 17.7ms, max memory growth 5.9MB, loading_state_seen_at_ms max 0.1ms, min Sigma canvas count 7, and every production record has a nonblank canvas or visible Sigma signal.

evidence: All tasks in the plan are done.
All phase acceptance checks are recorded in progress.
Full final acceptance command list passed sequentially.

Final artifact proves all 11 graph shapes satisfy hard gates: fps >=45, frame p95 <=22.3ms for wheel/drag, action durations within recorded thresholds, memory growth within threshold, loading-state/schema/threshold fields present, production_path=true, and 0 failed records.

Post-review artifact proves the real production path across all 11 graph shapes: 110 records, production_path=true, 0 failures, required schema/threshold/loading fields present, min fps 60.3, max frame p95 17.6ms, max memory growth 6MB, loading_state_seen_at_ms max 0ms, and no failed records.

Ship-review artifact proves the hardened production path across all 11 graph shapes: 110 records, production_path=true, 0 failures, min fps 60.2, max frame p95 17.7ms, max memory growth 5.9MB, loading_state_seen_at_ms max 0.1ms, min Sigma canvas count 7, and every production record has a nonblank canvas or visible Sigma signal.
final.residual_risk: Evidence is from local Chromium/Playwright runs. WebGL/Sigma availability can vary by user environment, so the implemented fallback policy remains necessary: small abnormal graphs use DOM/SVG emergency fallback and large abnormal graphs use aggregation safety fallback. Ship-review checks now prove the real production path with canvas count and rendered/visible Sigma signals, but browser/WebGL availability remains environment-dependent. No known unresolved product decision remains.
